title: US Senator Calls for Ban on Elected Officials Issuing Memecoins
summary: Senator Kirsten Gillibrand wants to prevent U.S. politicians from creating their own cryptocurrencies. The proposed restriction would apply to Congress members, the president, and their spouses.
body:
Senator Kirsten Gillibrand has introduced a proposal to ban U.S. elected officials from issuing or sponsoring their own digital assets. The restriction targets memecoins and other digital assets, aiming to prevent conflicts of interest. It would apply to members of Congress, the U.S. president, and their spouses.

The proposal comes amid growing concerns about the influence of cryptocurrencies in politics. Memecoins, in particular, have gained popularity due to their speculative nature and potential for quick profits. Senator Gillibrand's office has not yet provided additional details on how the ban would be enforced or the timeline for its implementation.

This move could have significant implications for the crypto community and political transparency. If passed, the law would help ensure that elected officials do not use digital assets to gain personal financial benefits at the expense of their public duties. It would also set a precedent for other countries considering similar regulations.

For everyday crypto users, this proposal highlights the increasing scrutiny on the intersection of politics and cryptocurrencies. While it may not directly impact most investors, it underscores the need for transparency and ethical behavior in the crypto space. Watch for updates on whether this proposal gains traction in Congress and how it might evolve.
twitterThread: Senator Kirsten Gillibrand wants to ban U.S. politicians from creating their own memecoins. This could set a major precedent for crypto in politics.
The proposed restriction would prevent Congress members, the president, and their spouses from issuing or sponsoring digital assets. Memecoins are often seen as speculative and risky.

This move highlights the growing scrutiny on crypto's role in politics. Stay tuned for updates on whether this ban gains traction. Read more → https://cryptocatalyst.news/articles/2026-07-03-senator-proposes-ban-on-elected-officials-issuing-memecoins
